sections.offgrid:
Based on current news and developments, the following items meet the reporting threshold for northern New Mexico off-grid and alternative housing:

**NM Solar Incentives – Federal Credit Elimination & State Credit Proposal**

President Trump signed legislation on July 4, 2025, eliminating the federal 30% home solar tax credit effective January 1, 2026, ten years before its scheduled expiration.

A 2026 NM Legislature bill proposes increasing the state solar tax credit from 10% to 30% with the cap raising from $6,000 to $15,000.

https://350newmexico.org/solar-and-battery-storage/

**Pangea Community Development – Taos Off-Grid Project**

Pangea Biotecture announced an agreement to acquire Tarleton Ranch, a 300+ acre property to be transformed into a groundbreaking sustainable and regenerative living development designed to honor Taos' heritage, reflecting commitment to affordable housing, cultural preservation, and ecological stewardship.

The development includes affordable homes eliminating utility costs through off-grid systems and sustainable infrastructure featuring solar, wind, micro-hydro energy, rainwater harvesting, and greywater recycling.

https://pangeabuild.com/pangea-community-a-visionary-off-grid-development-in-taos-new-mexico/

**Taos County Zoning Update – Land Use Regulations Revision**

Taos County hired Architectural Research Consultants, Inc. to revise and update Land Use Regulations (Ordinance 2018-2) and Subdivision Regulations (Ordinance 2005-8), aiming to modernize codes, simplify them into a single land development code, and encourage ecologically sound and sustainable development practices.

https://www.taoscounty.org/492/Land-Use-and-Subdivision-Regulations-Upd

No current news found for: NM CID modular/kit home updates, Taos-area off-grid community events with real event dates, or recent NM water rights/well drilling legislation changes.

The search results indicate general listings exist but lack the specific detailed pricing, acreage, and water/road information needed for the requested format. Let me conduct additional targeted searches for specific current listings.
Based on available search results, insufficient detailed listings matching all specified criteria (2+ acres, under $60,000, in exact target communities) were found with complete water/road access information. The searches reveal limited inventory of vacant land parcels in the specific target communities that meet all criteria simultaneously.

## Findings Summary

**Verified Listings Meeting Minimum Criteria:**

$39,000 | 2.49 acres | Arroyo Hondo (Mesa Sea Road) | Water: Electricity at lot | Road: County Road (CR B-007/Los Rios), gravel | Source: Land.com | URL: Mesa Sea Road, Arroyo Hondo, NM 87513

$59,000 | 2.49 acres | Arroyo Hondo (Mesa Sea Road) | Water: Electricity available | Road: County-maintained | Source: Zillow

Additional inventory exists but exceeds the $60,000 threshold. 
Taos County listings identified include $95,000 for 4 acres in El Prado; $70,000 for 3 acres in Ranchos de Taos; $65,000 for 1.24 acres in Ranchos de Taos; and $169,000 for 10 acres in Arroyo Hondo
.

**Market Trends:**

Land sales are down with more land for sale available; buyers can pick and choose, and are rarely paying full price.

Taos land market remains firmly buyer-favored due to elevated inventory.

Average listing price in Taos County is $444,193 with average cost per acre at $23,529.

Median price per acre in Taos County is $12,846.

**Limitations:** Searches across LandWatch, Land.com, Zillow, and Realtor.com yielded limited explicit results for 2+ acre parcels in specific target areas (Tres Piedras, Carson, Ojo Caliente, Hondo Mesa, Arroyo del Agua) under $60,000 with detailed utility/road specifications. Most listings of vacant land in the under-$60,000 range are <2 acres (typically 0.25-0.75 acre subdivided lots in Tres Piedras Estates and Carson Estates). The market predominantly features either small lots with owner-financing options or larger properties exceeding budget parameters.
sections.builders:
Based on available information, three of the specified builders present significant budget and feasibility challenges for the stated parameters. Here is the analysis:

## ZOOK CABINS (IRC Modular—Fully Assembled)

**Pricing & 2025-26 Changes:**
- 
Base pricing starts around $132,500 for the Rockwood model

- 
Pinecrest model starting at 100k with shipping included

- 
All park models include delivery cost at one price, avoiding additional transport fees

- No 2025-26 price escalation data located; company pricing structure remains tied to delivery ZIP code

**Customer Reviews:**
- Positive: 
Zook Cabins has a 4.8 star rating with 592 reviews
; 
overwhelmingly positive customer reviews praised high-quality craftsmanship, stress-free project management, skilled staff, family-friendly atmosphere, and durability for rental properties

- Negative: 
One verified complaint reported broken delivery promises on timeline with resort project—promised Memorial Day delivery broken twice; unresponsive company communication

**New Mexico Delivery:**
No explicit confirmation found. Company states 
service to 48 continental states
, but specific NM regional availability and delivery feasibility to Taos County at 7,000ft not documented.

**Model Status (Heritage, Grove ADU, Rivara ADU):**
These specific models not confirmed in current 2025-26 product lineup or pricing searchable on website. Company offers park models under 400sf, log cabins, and modern cabins, but these three names do not appear in recent materials.

## MIGHTY SMALL HOMES (SIP Panel Kit—DIY Assembly Required)

**Pricing & 2025-26 Changes:**
- 
Cost estimate $140–$328+ per square foot, all-inclusive (kit, labor, site work)

- 
ADU kits range $40,000–$120,000 depending on size and features; shipping costs extra and vary by location

- 
Expect about $4 per mile freight from Louisville, Kentucky, with minimum $1,200 charge

- No specific 2025-26 escalation announced

**Customer Reviews:**
- Limited independent third-party reviews found; 
Facebook page shows 92% recommend (10 reviews)

- 
Kits complete and ship in 6 weeks, or as little as 4 weeks with no customizations

- Company emphasizes energy efficiency but minimal negative feedback available

**New Mexico Delivery:**
- Likely available given national shipping from Kentucky, but no explicit Taos County confirmation
- 
Shipping $3/mile from Louisville KY
—approximately $3,000-4,000+ to Taos at ~1,100 miles

**Model Availability (A-Frame 506sf, Modern 600sf, Cottage 600sf, Ranch 768sf):**
- 
Buyers choose between 14 kits with styles including modern, contemporary, ranch, cottage, carriage homes; tiny homes on wheels also available

- Named models not explicitly verified in current active catalog

**Critical Constraint:**

Buyers are responsible for finishing the interior including electrical, plumbing, finishes like flooring and cabinetry and appliances
—significant additional cost and complexity at 7,000ft elevation.
